blockingIssues.description: `machine_name_from_config()` only reads `AppConfig.computer_name`, so `sync_beacon_push()` ignores the dedicated `syncBeaconMachineName` setting and publishes beacon data under the generic computer name or hostname instead of the user-configured beacon identity.

The payload sent from `packages/ui/src/lib/sync-beacon-context.tsx` hard-codes `lastCommitHash`, `lastCommitMessage`, and `lastCommitTimestamp` to empty values, so remote machines cannot display the required last-commit details in the Sync Beacon panel.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the `tauri-fullstack` skill to explicitly require checking parity between persisted frontend/shared config fields and Rust `AppConfig`/backend config structs whenever a feature adds or changes stored settings.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The sync-beacon backend review found that `packages/shared/src/types.ts` and frontend settings use `syncBeaconMachineName`, but `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands.rs` omitted the corresponding Rust config field and only used `computer_name`, causing the user-configured beacon identity to be ignored.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify in `tauri-fullstack` that browser-based verification is only a smoke test for Tauri-dependent features and cannot fully validate flows that require Tauri runtime commands; workers should document that limitation and rely on Rust-side validation where appropriate.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The sync-beacon frontend review and worker feedback both noted that Sync Beacon gist operations depend on Tauri invoke commands, so browser verification could confirm UI reachability but not end-to-end beacon behavior.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a library entry documenting that Sync Beacon Gist operations require Tauri runtime.
Add missing sync beacon command literals to the shared `MessageType` union.
rejectedObservations.reason: valid non-blocking code issue captured in feature review, but not a shared-state documentation or services update for scrutiny synthesis
